text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in a quaint little town called Whisperbrook, there lived an owl named Ook Ook. Ook rarely saw sunlight. Instead, he knew stars quite well. One particularly bright star caught his eye one night, causing him to swoop down from his high branch.

There sat Mary Lou. She had blue eyes like blueberries and hair like straw. Why didn twinkling? She had lost tonight's moon. Why wouldnecided?

So she danced. She spun like tops. She'd pause. Why shine!

Ook smiled. He'd always knew moonlight had charms.

Maybe I'll catch moonbeams.

So he did. Now they shine bright. Our friend.

One day, Owl Moore said, "Think big."

Ook did. He built a clock.

Now I'll count stars.

Ooky patterns. He did.

Whisperbrook got bright.

Once, Owl Moore fell.

Ook flew fast.

They found Moore.

Now know moons' friends.

That
